rulings.Case Title: SAMETC, LLC et al VS. TED AND PAULINE ELMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Prevailing Party And Fixing Attorneys Fees Recoverable As Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 20, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER SAMETC, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING PREVAILING PARTY AND FIXING ATTORNEYS FEES RECOVERABLE AS COSTS is continued to March 14, 2024. Moving party to file a supplemental brief, not to exceed 3 pages, addressing authorities cited in section B of opposition starting on page 13. Supplemental brief to be filed and served no later than March 1, 2024. No later than March 4, 2024 by 5:00 p.m. parties to deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion properly bound and tabbed with cover letters reflecting the new hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STEVE GRIFFITH ET AL VS. CRAIG WALKER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 20, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO ENFORCE TERMS OF SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff does not present admissible evidence sufficient to support a finding that Defendant has breached the terms of the parties' settlement agreement. (Cal. Evid. Code, Section 403, subd. (a)(2), 1200.). The Court notes three additional issues with the instant motion. First, Plaintiffs' requested relief-placing the matter back on the Court's trial calendar-is not provided for in the parties' settlement agreement. Second, it is unclear why Plaintiffs never dismissed this matter. The settlement agreement required Plaintiffs to dismiss the matter by August 1, 2023, if Defendant satisfied his obligations under the agreement. Plaintiffs do not argue Defendant failed to comply with the pre-August deadlines but nonetheless have failed to dismiss the case. Third, Plaintiffs seem to anticipate that this settlement is enforceable as long as Defendant resides at the subject property but give no authority supporting such proposition. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

DEFENDANT CAROL RITSCHELL'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Hearing Required.
